steps.title: Distribute each term of the first polynomial
steps.content:
Multiply $(2x^3 + 3x)$ by each term in $(x^3 - 2x)$:

$$2x^3(x^3 - 2x) + 3x(x^3 - 2x)$$

Expanding each product:
  • $2x^3 \cdot x^3 = 2x^6$
  • $2x^3 \cdot (-2x) = -4x^4$
  • $3x \cdot x^3 = 3x^4$
  • $3x \cdot (-2x) = -6x^2$

This gives us: $2x^6 - 4x^4 + 3x^4 - 6x^2$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
The $x^4$ terms combine: $-4x^4 + 3x^4 = -x^4$.

The final simplified expression is:
$$2x^6 - x^4 - 6x^2$$

That matches choice C — C is correct.
